name: Karaganda Region (Kazakhstan)

colors.symbolism: drawn from the Kazakh national flag — the sky and the heritage of the Turkic peoples

description: A blue field charged centrally with the regional emblem of Karaganda — drawing on the central Kazakh steppe heritage and the region's coal-mining industry.
history: Karaganda Region is one of the central regions of Kazakhstan and historically the country's principal coal-mining area. The regional flag was adopted after independence in 1991, alongside other Kazakh regional symbols.
